name: radial_array_parity_case1
source: Frozen reference-editor capture (toolcards/radial_array/capture/parity_case1_{before,after}.json in the private task worktree, task 0356, 2026-07-11). NOT replayed against any external engine at test time — the counts and step law below are the only facts pulled from that capture; vertex ORDER is vibe3d-native (the reference's internal insertion order is not reproduced).

provenance.notes: Frozen reference-editor capture (parity_case1_{before,after}.json, 2026-07-11). NOT replayed against any external engine at test time — only counts + the step law are pulled from the capture; vertex ORDER is vibe3d-native, not reference-order-matched.

description: 8v/6f unit cube, one face selected (the +Y top face, 4 verts at y=0.5). Params: Count=4, Axis=Y, Center=origin, End Angle=270deg (Start implicitly 0), Offset=0.3 (TOTAL span across the array, reference semantics), Weld=0 (merge off), Replace Source=off (kept). Measured result: +12 verts / +3 faces — Count=4 means 4 total array elements, the ORIGINAL top face IS the 0-degree element (replace=false), and 3 new clones fill the 90/180/270-degree positions. The 3 new clones' Y values step by +0.1 each (0.6/0.7/0.8), NOT a flat +0.3 per clone: offset/(count-1) = 0.3/3 = 0.1 — a TOTAL span distributed evenly across the (count-1) point-to-point intervals. This is the corrected offset law RadialArrayTool implements (extraShift = axisUnit * offset/(count-1)); the original pre-capture guess (offset * axisUnit per clone) was wrong.
